name: Community Life Center - Mount Pleasant Christian Church
description: Community Life Center - Mount Pleasant Christian Church is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Greenwood, IN. There are 6 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play. Reservations are not available. Players have access to food, restrooms, water, and locker rooms.
open_play_schedule: only 3 courts available from 7am-8am.  6 courts available at 8:00am with one court designated for casual play, if needed and one designated challenge court.
fees: Fee required — Free for members ($40/yr for ages 60+) or $5 facility day pass

name: Community Park
description: Community Park is a place to play pickleball in Franklin, IN. There are 6 outdoor asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. The facility includes amenities like restrooms.

name: Craig Park
description: Craig Park is a place to play pickleball in Greenwood, IN. There are 7 outdoor hard and concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. You can’t reserve the courts. The courts are free. Amenities include restrooms and water.

name: Drop Shot Pickleball
description: Drop Shot Pickleball is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Franklin, IN. There are 3 indoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, wheelchair accessibility, and lights. A membership is required to play. The courts can be reserved.
fees: Members only — Free Membership Options Available

name: Franklin Boys and Girls Club
description: Come play pickleball at Franklin Boys and Girls Club in Franklin, IN! There are 6 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. You can’t reserve the courts. A one-time fee is required to play. Amenities include restrooms and water.

name: Franklin Rec Center
description: Franklin Rec Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Franklin, IN. There are 4 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play. Reservations are available. Players have access to restrooms.

name: Freedom Springs
description: Come play pickleball at Freedom Springs in Greenwood, IN! There are 16 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. You can’t reserve the courts. The courts are free. Amenities include restrooms, water, and lights.

name: Greenwood Community Center
description: All Are Welcome for open play. For the latest information, join our group on the TeamReach app (code "GreenwoodPB") and "Pickleball Indy South" Facebook group
fees: Fee required — Free for Members and Renew Active.  Mon, Wed & Fri $4. Thurs. Eve $6 for Greenwood residents, $7 for non-residents.

name: Greenwood Fieldhouse
description: Greenwood Fieldhouse is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Greenwood, IN. There are 4 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play. Players have access to restrooms and water.
open_play_schedule: On Wednesday mornings only 3 courts will be available.  The practice court will be open to the public.
fees: Fee required — Free for members and Renew Active.  Tue, Wed and Thu - $5 drop-in.  Fri evening - $6 for Greenwood residents, $8 for non-residents.

name: Independence Park
description: Good parking, attached to a playground

description: Indianapolis Pickleball Club South is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Greenwood, IN. There are 8 indoor acrylic, hard, and concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include food, lights, a pro shop or equipment store, trainers or lessons, water, youth programming, and restrooms.

description: Come play pickleball at Pickle Grove in Bargersville, IN! There are 3 indoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to restrooms and lights. You can reserve the courts. A one-time fee is required to play.

description: Come play pickleball at PMMS in Frances, IN! There are 4 indoor courts. Tape lines have been laid, and portable nets are available. The courts are private.
